directive.title: Database connection layer with environment validation

auditor.summary: The generated code provides a solid foundation for the database connection layer with proper pooling, retry logic, circuit breaker, and audit logging. However, there are several critical issues that prevent this from being production-ready. The most significant problems are: (1) circular dependency risks from dynamic imports across multiple files, (2) missing health check implementation (lib/db/health.ts and /app/api/health/db/route.ts are not generated), (3) memory leak in circuit breaker timeout handling, and (4) potential build-time execution issues with env-validator. The code also has several medium-severity issues including type mismatches, inefficient dynamic imports, and Node.js-specific APIs that may not work in all serverless environments. The build is recoverable with fixes, hence CONDITIONAL verdict.

top_issues.description: Dynamic import of './audit-log' inside logEvent method creates a circular dependency risk and breaks the singleton pattern. The import is called asynchronously and may fail if audit-log.ts imports from circuit-breaker.ts.
Dynamic import of './audit-log' inside logRetryEvent function creates same circular dependency risk as circuit-breaker.ts.
Dynamic import of './circuit-breaker' inside pool error handler creates circular dependency risk.
